title: myosotis rakiura
intro: Myosotis rakiura, also known as Rakiura forget-me-not or Rakiura blue forget-me-not, is a flowering plant species endemic to New Zealand. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. The health benefits of Myosotis rakiura include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and soothe sore throats and coughs. Myosotis rakiura is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and flowers can be used fresh or dried for medicinal purposes.
